page_content:
ASTR690 Reasearch Project I (3 Credits)

ASTR615 Computational Astrophysics (3 Credits) Introduction to computational techniques used in astrophysical research. Topics include modern high performance computer architectures, scientiﬁc visualization and data analysis, and detailed descriptions of numerical algorithms for the solution to a wide range of mathematical systems important in astrophysics. Restriction: Permission of CMNS-Astronomy department. Credit Only Granted for: ASTR415 or ASTR615.

ASTR695 Introduction to Research (1 Credit) Provides an introduction to research programs in the Department of Astronomy and a forum to explore possible research projects. Aimed at incoming graduate students.
